response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is primarily a TypeScript/Node.js engineer with roughly 17 years of broad software experience, but his C++ exposure amounts to approximately six months at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project — far short of the 8–12 years of C++ depth this role demands. He has no industrial automation domain experience, no familiarity with IEC 62443 or Secure Development Lifecycle practices, and no exposure to MQTT or industrial protocols such as Modbus, OPC UA, or IEC 61850. While his distributed-systems and event-driven architecture work is genuinely strong, those skills do not compensate for the absence of the core technical requirements.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance distributed systems work handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby, showing scalability and low-latency design awareness
Hands-on event-driven architecture experience through the open-source GISCollective geo-data platform
Solid agile and software-craftsmanship practices including TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, and mentoring via workshops and dojos
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project); the role requires 8–12 years of C++ and STL expertise as its primary technical foundation
Zero industrial automation or IoT background; no exposure to Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, MQTT, or any industrial protocol
No evidence of Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) implementation or IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity standards, which are core requirements of the role
